item_xml:
<item>
      <title>Vijayanagara Chapter 4: The Empire Between Knives and Crowns</title>
      <description>This chapter follows Vijayanagara through the violent breakdown of the old Sangama order, the rise of Saluva Narasimha amid palace murder and dynastic uncertainty, the regency struggles associated with Narasa Nayaka and Vira Narasimha, the repeated Raichur-Bijapur contests, and the arrival of the Portuguese horse trade and Goa politics that reshaped peninsular power. It closes by showing how these unstable decades, though confused in the sources and soaked in intrigue, preserved enough of the empire for Krishna Deva Raya to inherit a state still capable of expansion and glory. Based on A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara) by Robert Sewell (1900). </description>
